goal: Show whether same-transaction liquidity manipulation can influence a price/status/liquidation decision.
minimal_state: protocol collateral/status decision depends on AMM or callback-reachable state
attacker_actions: manipulate pool balance or invoke flash-loan callback
call the target decision before state normalizes
assertions: decision cannot cross economically meaningful threshold from transient manipulation
common_failures: using too-small liquidity to prove non-exploit
not checking permanent side effect after transaction
notes: Derived from existing normalized Code4rena case reports and intended as a reusable validation scaffold, not a protocol-specific PoC.
